_meta.author: scorecard-refine-national autopilot
_meta.note: Evidence-finalize TX House HD79-83 batch: claudia-ordaz, don-mclaughlin, brooks-landgraf, tom-craddick, dustin-burrows. State tier. Cited per-cell; reset unspecified to null. High-value catches: Ordaz documented progressive (was party-default); Craddick corrected off a phony 100 (establishment fiscal F-record); McLaughlin Uvalde-connected R firmly anti-gun-restriction.

profile.confidence_note: Sitting HD79 (El Paso) rep since Jan 2023 (formerly 'Claudia Ordaz Perez'; old HD76 eliminated in 2021 redistricting); advanced from 2026-03-03 Democratic primary, faces Jesse Romero (R) 2026-11-03. Scored from authored bills + 89th-session roll calls (Texas Values Action) + pro-life-group ratings. Was party_default; now documented progressive social record.

claudia-ordaz@TX.notes_set: Sitting HD79 (El Paso) Democrat since Jan 2023 (formerly Claudia Ordaz Perez; HD76 was eliminated in 2021 redistricting and she won HD79 over fellow Dem Art Fierro). DOCUMENTED PROGRESSIVE SOCIAL RECORD: prime author of HB220 (89th) mandating abortifacient emergency contraception be offered to assault survivors over conscientious objection (Texas Right to Life OPPOSED); voted AGAINST SB33 (No Taxpayer Funds for Abortion travel); voted AGAINST HB229 'What Is a Woman Act'; voted to SUPPORT HB1738 (repeal of homosexual-conduct statute); voted AGAINST SB12 Parents' Bill of Rights; voted AGAINST SB10 (Ten Commandments). Texas Values Action 2025 rating 19%. CROSS-PARTY DEVIATION (conservative side): voted FOR SB11 (school prayer/Bible-reading time) -- a break from the Democratic line. Pattern of recorded ABSENCES on the marquee trans bills: absent on both SB14 (2023 youth gender-medicine) record votes and on SB1257 (2025 detransitioner bill). Texas Alliance for Life 65% reflects non-abortion 'life'/medical votes, not abortion stance. No documented conservative-aligned policy positions found beyond the SB11 prayer vote (which sits in a mixed cell with her Ten Commandments NO). Currency verified vs Ballotpedia + house.texas.gov.

profile.confidence_note: Freshman HD80 (Uvalde/Eagle Pass) rep since Jan 2025 (former Uvalde mayor during 2022 Robb Elementary shooting); advanced from 2026-03-03 GOP primary, faces Cecilia Castellano (D) 2026-11-03. Only the 2025 (89th) session of votes; scored from TAFL rating + SB2 vote + authored HB33 + cited first-person gun statements. Thin but clean record.

don-mclaughlin@TX.notes_set: Freshman HD80 (Uvalde/Eagle Pass, South Texas border district) Republican since Jan 2025; former Mayor of Uvalde during the 2022 Robb Elementary shooting. Endorsed by Trump, Abbott, Ted Nugent. Texas Alliance for Life 2025 96%. Voted YES SB2 (2025 ESA/voucher) and co-authored Abbott's voucher bill (hedged he won't 'gut my public school systems'). Authored HB33 'Uvalde Strong Act' mandating law-enforcement mass-shooting response plans/active-shooter drills/chain-of-command (passed House unanimously; no gun provisions). HIGH-VALUE 2A FINDING: as a Uvalde-connected Republican he FIRMLY OPPOSES gun restrictions -- explicitly rejected raising the rifle purchase age 18->21 ('if all we want to do is raise the age, that's not going to solve the problem'; 'someone determined to kill will not be deterred by gun laws'), a reversal from earlier mayoral openness; no gun-control in his Uvalde Strong Act. Texans for Fiscal Responsibility Fiscal Index 71 (above House avg 59, below the 90 'Champion' line) -- mid-conservative, not scored contrary. Breaks from TFR by OPPOSING a ban on city/taxpayer-funded lobbying (Uvalde water advocacy). Currency verified vs Ballotpedia + capitol.texas.gov.

4.note: 96%-rated pro-life R; no abortion-industry PAC funding found

profile.confidence_note: Sitting HD81 (Odessa/Permian Basin) rep since 2015, chairs Environmental Regulation; unopposed in 2026 GOP primary, faces Ceasar Sanchez (D) general. Scored from JBS Freedom Index roll calls + Texas Values Action 89th votes + pro-life-group ratings + authored bills. Solid social conservative; establishment fiscal knock + 2023 voucher flip.

brooks-landgraf@TX.notes_set: Sitting HD81 (Odessa/Ector County, Permian Basin) Republican since 2015; chairs House Environmental Regulation. Texas Alliance for Life 2025 100%, Texas Right to Life 83; voted YES SB8 (heartbeat), SB33, SB1233. Texas Values Action 87%: voted YES HB229 'What Is a Woman Act', YES SB1257 (detransitioners), YES SB13 (library books), NO HB1738 (kept homosexual-conduct statute), YES SB11 (school prayer), PRESENT then YES SB10 (Ten Commandments). Voted YES HB796 (Texas Sovereignty Act), YES HB1927 (permitless carry); coauthored HB2622 (2A Sanctuary State); NRA 'A' + endorsement; TSRA PAC endorsement. Authored HB219 'Verified Citizen Voting Act' (proof-of-citizenship registration). Coauthored HB20 (2021 bail reform barring personal bonds for violent offenses). 2023 VOUCHER FLIP: one of 4 named rural-R holdouts who voted with Democrats on the April 2023 anti-voucher vote, then flipped pro-voucher in Nov 2023; supported SB2 in 2025 (served as acting speaker during floor debate, publicly defended ESAs) -- drew an Abbott primary endorsement. ESTABLISHMENT FISCAL RECORD: Texans for Fiscal Responsibility career C- (F in 2017/2019/2021/2023); JBS Freedom Index collapsed to 40% (2025-26) from 83% (2019-20). Also voted NO on HJR98 (Article V/Convention of States). Currency verified vs house.texas.gov + Ballotpedia.

profile.confidence_note: Sitting HD82 (Midland) rep since 1969 (longest-serving member in Texas House history; former Speaker 2003-09); going for a 30th term, no retirement announced, running 2026-03-03 primary. Scored from TAFL rating + voucher roll calls + Freedom Index + Texas Values. Corrected off a prior phony 100 -- documented establishment fiscal record.

tom-craddick@TX.notes_set: Sitting HD82 (Midland) Republican since 1969 -- longest-serving member in Texas House history and longest-serving sitting state legislator in the U.S.; former Speaker (2003-2009); major oil-and-gas figure. Going for a 30th term in 2026 (no retirement announced). Texas Alliance for Life 2025 100%. Voted NO HB1738 (one of the majority who kept the homosexual-conduct statute -- not among the 12 R who crossed to repeal). VOUCHERS: voted YEA SB2 (2025; only Phelan & VanDeaver were GOP NOs) and voted pro-voucher in 2023 (against the Raney anti-voucher amendment). Freedom Index lists him as supporting permitless carry (HB1927). ESTABLISHMENT FISCAL RECORD (the correction): Texans for Fiscal Responsibility career C- with straight F grades in 2017/2019/2021/2023; JBS Freedom Index conservatism halved to 50% (2023-24 & 2025-26) from 83% (2019-20); voted FOR HB2 (~$8.5B education appropriation) and housing/music-venue subsidies; voted NO on HJR98 (Article V/Convention of States) and HB4217 (eminent domain). Prior record showed an unsupportable 100/100; this finalization scores the documented record. NOTE: prior page carried a Tennessee TSRA mis-citation -- replaced. Currency verified vs Ballotpedia + house.texas.gov.

profile.confidence_note: Speaker of the Texas House (77th, elected Jan 2025) representing HD83 (Lubbock), rep since 2015; advanced from 2026-03-03 GOP primary, faces Malik Williams (D) 2026-11-03. Speaker-vote tradition limits 2025 individual roll calls; scored from bills championed as Speaker + pre-Speaker roll calls + ratings + factional record.

dustin-burrows@TX.notes_set: Speaker of the Texas House (elected Jan 2025) representing HD83 (Lubbock); Republican rep since 2015. SPEAKER-VOTE WRINKLE: by House tradition the presiding officer usually does not cast recorded floor votes, so a low 2025 roll-call count is procedural, not ideological -- most HARD personal votes below are pre-Speaker (2015-2024). As Speaker he CHAMPIONED SB2 (2025 universal ESA/voucher) -- publicly drove passage and spoke at signing -- and his House passed SB10 (Ten Commandments), SB11 (school prayer), SJR5 (bail reform, 133-8; later Prop 3) and HB796 (Texas Sovereignty Act). Co-sponsor of SB1 (2021 Election Integrity Act); named House conferee on HB1927 (2021 permitless carry). Texas Alliance for Life credited him by name for 2025 pro-life wins. FACTIONAL / ESTABLISHMENT CATCH: won the speakership with a Republican-Democrat coalition over hard-right favorite David Cook (cast as 'a blow to the GOP's hard right'); RPT threatened censure of members who backed him; branded 'RINO' by grassroots; appointed Democrat committee chairs; 2019 'Burrowsgate' secret-recording episode cost him the House GOP Caucus chair. Texas Right to Life criticized him for the death of priority abortion-pill bill SB2880 ('the buck stops with the Speaker'). JBS Freedom Index career 61% (50% in 2023-24) corroborates the establishment framing -- offset by his authorship of the 2019 property-tax-cap SB2. Currency verified vs Ballotpedia + house.texas.gov.

0.note: Texas Alliance for Life credited Speaker Burrows by name for 2025 pro-life victories; long pro-life voting career. (TRTL criticized SB2880 priority -- noted, but not a pro-abortion position)
